---
title: "Formula Sheet"
subtitle: "Introduction to Particle Physics · Baikal School"
lang: en
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
---

# Counting Experiment

$$
N = \Phi \sigma N_{\rm target}T\epsilon
$$

Toy meaning only: this formula omits spectra, backgrounds, detector response, and uncertainty propagation.

# Decay Width and Lifetime

$$
\Gamma = \frac{1}{\tau}
$$

For an exponential decay law:

$$
P(t)=e^{-t/\tau}.
$$

# Natural Units

$$
\hbar=c=1
$$

Useful conversion:

$$
\hbar c \simeq 197.3\ \mathrm{MeV\,fm}.
$$

# Standard Model Gauge Group

$$
SU(3)_c\times SU(2)_L\times U(1)_Y
$$

# Higgs Vacuum Expectation Value

$$
\langle H\rangle = \frac{v}{\sqrt{2}},
\quad
v\simeq 246\ \mathrm{GeV}
$$

# Fermion Masses

$$
m_f = \frac{y_fv}{\sqrt{2}}
$$

This parameterizes charged-fermion masses through Yukawa couplings; it does not explain the numerical pattern of those couplings.

# Flavor and Mass States

$$
|\nu_\alpha\rangle=\sum_i U_{\alpha i}^*|\nu_i\rangle
$$

# Two-Flavor Oscillation Probability

$$
P_{\nu_\alpha\to\nu_\beta}
=
\sin^2 2\theta
\sin^2
\left(
1.27
\frac{\Delta m^2[\mathrm{eV}^2]L[\mathrm{km}]}
{E[\mathrm{GeV}]}
\right)
$$

Survival probability:

$$
P_{\nu_\alpha\to\nu_\alpha}
=
1-P_{\nu_\alpha\to\nu_\beta}.
$$
